output:
## Solution

Herder *i*'s profit is:

$$\pi_i = 500 \, n_i \left(1 - \frac{n_i + N_{-i}}{200}\right)$$

Taking the derivative with respect to $n_i$ and setting it to zero:

$$500\left(1 - \frac{2n_i}{200} - \frac{N_{-i}}{200}\right) = 0$$

Imposing symmetry ($n_i = n$, so $N_{-i} = 4n$):

$$1 - \frac{2n}{200} - \frac{4n}{200} = 0 \implies 1 - \frac{6n}{200} = 0 \implies n = \frac{200}{6} \approx 33.33$$

**Each herder grazes 33 cattle at Nash Equilibrium.**

This gives a total of ~167 cattle — far above the optimal 100 — illustrating the Tragedy of the Commons: individual rationality leads to collective overgrazing, dropping per-cow revenue from $250 to ~$83.
